title: Echoes of the Medina
medium: Mixed media on canvas, natural pigments, gold leaf
story: Inspired by the labyrinthine alleys of Fez's old medina, this piece translates the sensory experience of wandering through centuries of history. The gold leaf catches light differently at every hour, much like the changing moods of the ancient city.

title: Henna Dreams III
medium: Henna, natural pigments on handmade paper
story: Part of Al-Fassi's celebrated Henna Series, this work elevates the traditional art of Mehndi to monumental scale. The intricate patterns tell stories of celebration, womanhood, and North African identity.

title: Atlantic Light
medium: Oil and natural pigments on linen
story: The light of the Atlantic coast at Asilah — that particular silver-gold quality at sunset — is captured in layers of translucent pigment. The piece shifts in appearance throughout the day as natural light changes.

title: Concrete Gardens #7
story: Chaâbia markets are the true gardens of Moroccan cities. This image captures the unexpected beauty of a Casablanca market where vegetables, textiles, and sunlight create a living mosaic.

title: The Blue Divide
story: Chefchaouen's famous blue walls become an abstract seascape in this double-exposure photograph. The boundary between earth and sky, reality and reflection dissolves into pure color.

title: Tangier Interlude
story: The liminal space of Tangier's port — where Africa meets Europe, where travelers arrive and depart. The long exposure transforms the busy harbor into a contemplative study of movement and stillness.

title: Zellij Cascade
story: Thousands of hand-cut ceramic tiles flow down the wall like a waterfall frozen in time. Traditional Moroccan zellij patterns are deconstructed and reformed into a new visual language — one that honors the past while leaping into the future.

title: Roots in Transit
medium: Mixed media installation: ceramic, copper, silk thread
story: This suspended installation represents the artist's experience of living between cultures. Copper roots reach downward while ceramic leaves reach upward, connected by delicate silk threads that catch light like spider silk in morning dew.

title: Silent Geometry
story: An interactive floor installation where white ceramic geometric forms cast shadows that change throughout the day. Visitors become part of the artwork as their own shadows merge with the evolving patterns.
